seo.title: No-Code / Low-Code Platforms implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping No-Code / Low-Code Platforms projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for transitioning a no-code or low-code application from a development prototype to a production environment. It focuses on data integrity, security configurations, and workflow reliability across platforms like Bubble, Xano, and Make.

items.title: Define Primary Keys and Relationships
items.description: Verify that every table has a unique identifier and that cross-platform relationships (e.g., Airtable to Xano) are mapped using stable IDs rather than names or labels.

items.title: Implement Server-Side Validation
items.description: Ensure that data validation logic is enforced at the database level (e.g., Xano/Supabase constraints) rather than just on the visual front-end (e.g., Bubble/Webflow forms).

items.title: Configure Automated Backups
items.description: Enable daily automated snapshots of all primary databases and test a restoration process to verify data recovery time objectives.

items.title: Audit Data Redundancy
items.description: Identify and remove duplicate data entry points between the visual builder and the external backend to prevent synchronization conflicts.

items.title: Set Up Data Export Scripts
items.description: Create a recurring script or automated workflow to export critical business data into a platform-agnostic format (CSV/JSON) to mitigate vendor lock-in.

items.title: Enable Row-Level Security (RLS)
items.description: Configure database rules so that users can only access or modify records associated with their specific User ID.

items.title: Secure API Keys and Secrets
items.description: Move all third-party API keys (OpenAI, Stripe, SendGrid) from front-end elements to server-side environment variables or secret managers.

items.title: Disable Public API Access
items.description: Verify that all database endpoints are private by default and require a valid Bearer token or API key for access.

items.title: Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
items.description: Enable MFA for all administrative accounts on the primary no-code platform and connected automation tools.

items.title: Audit Plugin Permissions
items.description: Review the permissions of all installed third-party plugins to ensure they do not have unnecessary access to user data or private keys.

items.title: Optimize Asset Delivery
items.description: Compress all images to WebP format and host videos on dedicated CDNs rather than uploading directly to the no-code builder's internal storage.

items.title: Implement List Pagination
items.description: Configure all repeating groups or lists to use pagination or 'load more' functionality to prevent browser crashes on large datasets.

items.title: Minimize API Round-Trips
items.description: Consolidate multiple sequential API calls into a single backend function or 'Single Source of Truth' endpoint to reduce latency.

items.title: Set Up Conditional Loading
items.description: Configure visual elements to only render or fetch data when they are visible in the user's viewport.

items.title: Monitor Platform Capacity
items.description: Check the platform's 'Workflow Units' or 'Capacity' dashboard and set alerts for when usage exceeds 80% of the plan limit.

items.title: Implement Error Handling Paths
items.description: Add 'On Error' branches in Make/Zapier/n8n to catch failed steps and trigger notifications to the engineering team.

items.title: Use Idempotency Keys
items.description: Ensure that payment or order creation workflows use unique keys to prevent duplicate transactions if a workflow is retried.

items.title: Validate Webhook Signatures
items.description: Verify that incoming webhooks from external providers include a valid signature to prevent spoofing attacks.

items.title: Configure Dead-Letter Queues
items.description: Set up a dedicated table or log to store data from failed automation runs for manual re-processing.

items.title: Test Workflow Rate Limits
items.description: Simulate peak load to ensure that automation steps do not trigger rate limits on third-party APIs.

items.title: Isolate Environments
items.description: Confirm that Development, Staging, and Production environments use separate databases and API keys.

items.title: Document Custom Code Snippets
items.description: Maintain a central repository (e.g., GitHub or Notion) for all custom CSS, JavaScript, or SQL used within the no-code platform.

items.title: Set Up Uptime Monitoring
items.description: Configure external monitoring (e.g., UptimeRobot or Better Stack) for the application URL and critical API endpoints.

items.title: Review Legal and Compliance
items.description: Verify that data storage locations (e.g., AWS US-East vs EU-West) comply with regional data residency requirements like GDPR.

items.title: Final Domain and SSL Verification
items.description: Confirm that the custom domain is correctly routed and that the SSL certificate is active and auto-renewing.
